justification: All 7 required business rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Notification rule (BR-7) correctly marked invariant: false. Sanctions rule (BR-5) correctly marked invariant: true. Timezone/daily-limit ambiguity explicitly surfaced as an escalation. 15 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 55 is slightly below the 60-85 range specified in the rubric, but the reasoning is thorough and the lower confidence is defensible given the number of ambiguities identified. Additionally, Bird went beyond requirements by identifying T+1 business day ambiguity, sanctions screening directionality gap, and partially-verified withdrawal ambiguity.

observations.text: All 7 business rules correctly identified with accurate invariant classifications — BR-3 (daily limits amounts) and BR-7 (notification) as false, rest as true
BR-7 notification rule explicitly and correctly marked invariant: false with clear justification distinguishing UX failure from compliance violation
BR-5 sanctions screening included as invariant: true with strong legal justification referencing OFAC
Daily limit timezone ambiguity surfaced prominently in both edge_cases and escalations with concrete options (rolling 24h vs calendar day, UTC vs local)
15 acceptance criteria with well-structured Given/When/Then format, far exceeding the 4 minimum
BR-8 correctly distinguishes partially-verified from unverified accounts, avoiding the conflation failure mode
Excellent edge cases including the $1,000 boundary condition, sanctions priority over notification, and Friday T+1 ambiguity
Rich escalations section with actionable options and recommendations for each ambiguity
Confidence level of 55 is below the rubric's 60-85 range — while the lower score is arguably justified by the many ambiguities found, it technically falls outside the expected band
BR-3 makes a nuanced distinction: the existence of tiered limits is invariant but the specific dollar amounts are soft config — this is sophisticated domain reasoning

grader_results.detail: field confidence.level value 55 is below min 60
